description: Ryan Votta found the bird on the morning of January 19th, at approximately 7:15 AM. He called me to say he had a very rufous short-tailed Troglodytes calling like a WIWA, and he believed he had heard, but not identified, the same individual the prior morning. Around 9:40 AM I finally arrived at the location, where I met with Ryan, who stated he hadn't seen or heard the bird in some time.  After perhaps 45 minutes of searching I left. At 1230 Ryan called me to say he had relocated and photographed the bird. When I arrived, the photos he showed me didn't convince me, as the photos made the bird look a bit more cold (albeit through the viewfinder of an older camera) but we were able to relocate the bird and the overall rufous or rusty color of the bird stood out at my first glimpse. I was able to watch the bird for only perhaps 5-10 seconds bouncing around in the woody debris - (quick looks at about 10 ft through 10x42s of this bird). Because Ryan had obtained photos of the bird, my priority was to obtain audio of the bird calling, which I did successfully at rather low quality and for only a handful of notes.  In this recording, the call notes of the bird appear to resemble a Wilson's Warbler rather than a Song Sparrow.
